nodes.context: 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada.
One of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, Ontario.
One of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, Ontario.
Mafia family based in Buffalo, New York.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Musitano crime family and various gangs in Hamilton
edges.context: The Musitano family is listed as a rival of the Papalia family in the infobox.
The Buffalo crime family is listed as an ally of the Papalia family in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The killings of Johnny and his lieutenant Carmen Barillaro in 1997, ordered by the Musitanos, had effectively wiped out the family's remaining leaders in Canada.
edges.context: The Musitano family ordered the murder of Johnny Papalia and his lieutenant, leading to the decline of the Papalia family.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s, Papalia lieutenant Enio "Pegleg" Mora borrowed $7.2 million from Montreal mob boss Vito Rizzuto, and gave the bulk of the money to Papalia to open an upscale restaurant and nightclub in Toronto. After the Rizzuto crime family were not re-paid, in September 1996, Mora was shot in the head four times at a Vaughan farm; Giacinto Arcuri was arrested and charged with Mora's murder, but was acquitted for lack of evidence.
edges.context: The Papalia family borrowed money from the Rizzuto family and failed to repay, leading to the murder of a Papalia lieutenant.

edges.evidence_quote: Reflecting the new power structure, Gerald "Skinny" Ward of Welland, who had long served as the Papalia's family drug distributor and smuggler in the Niagara Peninsula area, went over to the Hells Angels after Johnny's murder.
edges.context: After Johnny Papalia's murder, a former Papalia associate switched allegiance to the Hells Angels.
